key_insight: The paper identifies a single "cliff token" that often triggers a model’s transition from correct reasoning to failure, and shows that removing it can substantially recover performance.
engineering_relevance: For LLM reasoning systems, pinpointing and mitigating one high-risk token offers a practical way to improve reliability, trace failures, and target training interventions more efficiently.
method: Define cliff tokens as positions where token-wise potential drops significantly under an adaptive local threshold using a one-sided two-proportion z-test.
Classify cliffs into deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off types based on greedy choice and token entropy.
Validate with Cliff-DPO, a single-token preference optimization at cliff positions.
evidence_signal: Evaluated across seven models and three math benchmarks (GSM1K, MATH500, AIME 2025); deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovered pass@64 to 1.0, and Cliff-DPO improved accuracy by up to +6.6.
watch_item: The approach depends on identifying cliff positions accurately, so thresholding and entropy-based taxonomy choices may affect robustness across tasks, prompts, and model families.
paper_title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
